summary: Justin Brennan coaches on mindset, capital raising fundamentals, and deal-sourcing processes for Brian's team. While not directly analyzing specific deals, the session covers critical operational foundations (LLC setup, website presence, CRM systems, investor list building) and mindset techniques (daily vision prayer, neuroplasticity, pain vs. pleasure motivation) that directly enable deal sourcing and underwriting capacity.
takeaways: Write and read a daily vision prayer twice daily (morning/evening) in present tense, specifying: (1) exact monthly income target with date, (2) income sources (job, multifamily, other), (3) giving-back plan; brain doesn't distinguish fiction from truth, so frequencies reprogram subconscious
Build 25-person CRM contact list within 48 hours with names, phone, emails to start capital attraction pipeline—these become soft commitments for future deal offerings

Set up LLC, professional website with advisory team section (include Brennan Polley Group as strategic partner), and CRM system before raising capital—'look the part' to gain broker/lender/investor confidence

When negative thoughts spiral, use three remedies in sequence: (1) state 3 things grateful for, (2) go barefoot outside to ground body and absorb vitamin D, (3) play 3-6 pre-selected positivity songs that reliably shift energy
Broker fees negotiated into deal price, not added to buyer costs—typical listing broker commission 4-6%, so negotiate buyer broker down to 2-2.5% to be paid from listing side

First deal can accept investment minimums below $25K (15-20K acceptable) but set baseline at $25K for LP management simplicity; use 506(b) offering (allows non-accredited investors, cannot publicly market) until volume justifies 506(c)

Use pain/pleasure motivation strategically—post-date accountability checks ($5-10K) to broker/mentor if deadline (e.g., December 31 deal lock) missed; fear-based motivation drives some personalities harder than pleasure incentives

skill_candidates.change: Establish investor pipeline before first deal by compiling 25-contact CRM list and attending 5+ local meetups to create soft commitments
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin emphasizes 'more hands you shake, the more money you make' and describes meeting people at meetup.com groups in adjacent industries (doctors, dentists, attorneys) who have capital and tax-avoidance incentives; soft commitments materialize into 25K+ investments when deal email template sent

skill_candidates.change: Pre-deal underwriting assumptions should incorporate negotiated broker fees (2-2.5% from listing side) rather than adding to buyer acquisition costs
skill_candidates.evidence: Tony asked if buyer broker NDA at 3% commission should adjust closing costs; Justin clarified fee comes from listing broker's 4-6% commission pool, gets negotiated into LOI/docs, and should be 2-2.5% not 3%

skill_candidates.change: Institute daily accountability for deal sourcing and underwriting by posting-dating penalty check ($5-10K) to mentor/broker with December 31 deal-lock deadline
skill_candidates.evidence: Gideon offered $3K check; Justin pushed to $10K post-dated check (referencing his own $10K post-dated check during real estate sales career that drove consistent action despite fear) to create pain-based motivation and 'consistent discipline' to stay in broker calls, deal review, and LOI submission cycle

title: Weekly Coaching_09-04-25 | Justin Brennan
